title: Emphasis & Intensifiers
subtitle: How to stress the idea that matters without sounding loud
intro: Emphasis is about controlled loudness. A Band 6 writer reaches for 'very', 'really', and 'a lot' — the blunt instruments of English. Band 8 writers use a richer toolkit: precise intensifiers, rhetorical repetition, do-auxiliary stress, and fronted adverbials. Emphasis done well signals conviction; overdone, it signals desperation.

rules.heading: Gradable vs. ungradable intensifiers
rules.body: Gradable adjectives (big, difficult, important) → very, extremely, highly, profoundly, considerably. Ungradable/absolute adjectives (impossible, essential, crucial, vital) → absolutely, utterly, completely, entirely. Don't write 'very essential' — write 'absolutely essential' or just 'essential'.

rules.heading: Do / does / did for verb emphasis
rules.body: 'Governments do recognise the problem, even if they are slow to act.' The auxiliary carries stress and often signals a contrast with an earlier claim.

rules.heading: Fronted emphasis and restrictive adverbs
rules.body: 'Only in exceptional circumstances should this approach be considered.' 'Particularly concerning is the lack of transparency.' These shift weight to the fronted element.

rules.heading: Emphatic vocabulary upgrades
rules.body: 'important' → pivotal, decisive, instrumental, of paramount importance. 'bad' → detrimental, deleterious, pernicious. 'big' → substantial, considerable, profound, far-reaching. Precision is emphasis.

examples.sentence: It is absolutely essential — not merely desirable — that regulators keep pace with technological change.
The findings do, however, cast doubt on the prevailing orthodoxy.
Particularly striking is the speed with which public opinion has shifted.
Renewable energy is not just cheaper — it is strategically indispensable.
Of paramount importance is the principle that no policy should exacerbate existing inequalities.

band8_marker: Band 8 writers match intensifier to adjective: 'profoundly influential', 'starkly illustrated', 'markedly different', 'decidedly inadequate'. They avoid the lazy 'very' and 'really'. They also use 'indeed' and 'in fact' to confirm and extend a point ('The reform was controversial; indeed, it sparked the largest protests in a decade') — a small move that subtly lifts register.

common_errors.fix: Absolute adjectives take 'absolutely' or no intensifier → 'absolutely essential' / 'perfect' / 'unique'.
Replace with precise alternatives: 'very important' → 'crucial'; 'really difficult' → 'formidable'.
One precise intensifier or a stronger adjective.

common_errors.error: Emphatic 'do' where the original is already strong: 'I do believe strongly that…'
common_errors.fix: Pick one emphatic device; 'do' is for contrastive emphasis.

practice.title: Upgrade the emphasis
practice.instruction: Rewrite to make the emphasis more academic.

items.prompt: This is a very important issue.
items.answer: This is an issue of considerable importance. / This is a pivotal issue.

items.prompt: Young people are really affected by social media.
items.answer: Young people are profoundly affected by social media. / Social media has a profound impact on young people.

items.prompt: The results were very different from what we expected.
items.answer: The results differed markedly from expectations. / The results were strikingly different from what had been anticipated.

items.prompt: It is very essential to act now.
items.answer: It is absolutely essential to act now. / Urgent action is indispensable.

items.prompt: Governments really need to invest in education. (Use emphatic 'do')
items.answer: Governments do need to invest in education.

items.prompt: The shift in public opinion has been very fast. (Front the striking adjective)
items.answer: Particularly striking is the speed of the shift in public opinion.

items.prompt: Transparency is the only thing that can restore trust. (Use cleft + emphasis)
items.answer: It is transparency, and transparency alone, that can restore trust.

items.prompt: This approach is very wrong. (Upgrade 'wrong')
items.answer: This approach is fundamentally flawed. / … deeply misguided.

items.prompt: A) 'Climate change is a really big problem.' B) 'Climate change is one of the defining challenges of our era.'
A) 'Reforms do matter, especially when resources are scarce.' B) 'Reforms matter a lot, especially when resources are scarce.'
A) 'It is very difficult to predict the outcome.' B) 'Predicting the outcome is notoriously difficult.'
A) 'Particularly concerning is the decline in biodiversity.' B) 'The decline in biodiversity is very concerning.'
A) 'The minister said strongly that the policy would not change.' B) 'The minister was categorical that the policy would not change.'
A) 'The impact is very significant.' B) 'The impact is far-reaching.'
A) 'It is incredibly rare to find consensus on this issue.' B) 'Consensus on this issue is exceedingly rare.'
A) 'The result was not bad.' B) 'The result was, in fact, quite encouraging.'

practice.title: Pick the precise intensifier
practice.instruction: Fill in with a single intensifier or emphatic marker.

items.prompt: The reform was ___ influential, reshaping every subsequent policy in the sector.
Such an approach is ___ inadequate in a crisis of this scale.
Timely intervention is ___ essential.
The findings were ___ different from earlier studies.
Public trust ___ matters in the delivery of vaccination programmes. (emphatic auxiliary)
The proposal is ___ of paramount importance to future generations.
The policy has proven ___ successful in reducing unemployment.
The link between income and health is ___ well documented.
